items.briefing:
A recent study found most organisations have already experienced AI-related security incidents, highlighting growing risks as AI tools become more common in workplaces. Separately, research shows Australian individuals face a relatively lower risk of cybercrime compared to other countries — however, pressure is shifting toward small and medium businesses (SMBs), which are increasingly being targeted and may have fewer resources to respond.

If your organisation uses AI tools, take a moment to review what data staff are sharing with them. Avoid entering sensitive student, staff, or client information into AI platforms unless your organisation has explicitly approved them for that purpose.

featured.title: Most firms hit by AI security incidents, study finds
featured.quote: Most Australian organisations exposed to AI security breaches, requiring immediate defensive action.
